common.appFullName: ASTRAEA - Adaptive Smart Traffic Regulation and Analysis for Efficient Automation

auth.loginSubtitle: Enter your credentials to access the dashboard
auth.registerSubtitle: Create an account to access the system

intersections.title: Intersections
intersections.name: Intersection Name
intersections.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this intersection?

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Traffic & Queue Analytics
analytics.noDataAvailable: No data available for this period
analytics.pageDescription: Analyze vehicle volume, queue, and light effectiveness from DynamoDB without dummy data.
analytics.aboutDescription: Vehicle volume is calculated based on vehicle counter increases, not by summing all telemetry. Level 0 means smooth, Level 1 means moderate queue, and Level 2 means heavy queue. All dates and hourly groupings use WIB.

users.title: Users
users.name: Name
users.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this user?

settings.title: Settings
settings.autoModeDesc: System will control traffic lights automatically.
settings.timezoneDesc: Data is stored in UTC in the database. Timezone only affects dashboard display.
settings.browserNotificationsDesc: Show alerts in browser when queue is congested or device is offline.
settings.emailNotificationsDesc: Send important alert summary to account email.
settings.telegramNotificationsDesc: Send important alerts to Telegram bot.
settings.iotConfigNote: Light timing and adaptive mode settings are still configured through the IoT Config page to sync with ESP32.
settings.secureLoginDesc: Account uses NextAuth. Local password is bcrypt encrypted, while Google account uses OAuth.
settings.passwordNote: Note: this feature only applies to email/password accounts. Google accounts do not have local passwords.

profile.title: Profile
profile.confirmDeleteAvatar: Are you sure you want to delete avatar?
profile.exportDescription: User exported profile data in JSON format
profile.skillsPlaceholder: Example: Traffic Management, IoT Systems, Data Analysis
profile.skillsSeparator: Separate each skill with a comma.
profile.responseTimeDesc: Response speed based on user activity gaps
profile.accuracyDesc: Successful activity ratio based on activity log
profile.efficiencyDesc: Activity efficiency based on user active hours
profile.performanceUnavailableDesc: Performance will appear automatically after user activities are recorded.
profile.publicProfileDesc: Allow others to view your profile
profile.showEmailDesc: Email will be visible on public profile
profile.showActivityDesc: Activity will be visible on public profile
profile.noActivityDesc: User activities will appear after actions in the system.

configPanel.title: Device Configuration
configPanel.subtitle: Configuration saved to DynamoDB and sent to ESP32 via Mosquitto MQTT.
configPanel.manualGreenDesc: Used when Adaptive Mode is disabled.
configPanel.savingError: An error occurred while saving configuration
configPanel.saveSuccess: Configuration saved successfully and sent to ESP32
configPanel.savePartialSuccess: Configuration saved, but some MQTT topics failed to send

help.title: Help
help.systemGuideDesc: Complete guide for using and configuring Adaptive Traffic Monitoring System

errors.invalidFileType: Invalid file type. Use images (jpg, png, etc)

timeFilter.startDateMustBeBeforeEnd: Start date must not be greater than end date
timeFilter.selectDateRangeToView: Select date range to view historical data. Maximum 90 days back.

trafficTrend.title: Traffic Trend & Queue Level
trafficTrend.vehicleCountNote: Total vehicles based on highest counter per lane.

modals.addUserDesc: Create account for operator or admin
modals.addIntersectionDesc: Connect intersection with detected ESP32 device
modals.deviceInfo: Device appears after ESP32 sends telemetry to MQTT. One device can only be connected to one intersection.
modals.noDevicesAvailable: No devices available. Make sure ESP32 is sending telemetry or use manual input.

charts.greenDurationSubtitle: Comparison of target green duration with average actual duration.
charts.noTelemetryFound: No telemetry found in selected period and filters.
charts.queueEffectivenessSubtitle: Percentage of adaptive system success in reducing queue time.
charts.queueByHourSubtitle: Queue pattern throughout the day to identify peak hours.
charts.queueDistributionSubtitle: Percentage of time at each queue level.
charts.vehicleVolumeSubtitle: Number of vehicles passing through intersections daily.

trafficControl.title: Real-time Traffic Control
trafficControl.subtitle: Adaptive traffic light control simulation
trafficControl.disableAutoModeToControl: Disable Auto Mode to control lights
trafficControl.disableAutoModeFirst: Disable Auto Mode before controlling lights manually
trafficControl.durationSettingsDesc: Values will be saved to DynamoDB and sent to ESP32 via MQTT retained
trafficControl.adaptiveModeDesc: Green duration follows traffic density level

roadSimulation.title: Traffic Simulation
roadSimulation.subtitle: Real-time traffic condition visualization
roadSimulation.info: Virtual cars only appear when actual vehicleCount increases. Red lights make cars stop and form queues; green lights make queues pass through intersections.

team.title: Our Team
team.subtitle: Meet the ASTRAEA development team
team.heroTitle: Building the Future of Smart Mobility.
team.heroSubtitle: Our engineers, designers, and analysts collaborate to create predictive and efficient traffic management solutions for smart cities.
team.footerDescription: Our team works with the highest security standards and data privacy protocols to ensure the ASTRAEA system is safe for all citizens.
team.joinDescription: We are constantly looking for innovators, engineers, and creative thinkers to help us revolutionize urban mobility.

landing.heroSubtitle: IoT Technology for more efficient traffic management
landing.heroDescription: ASTRAEA leverages the power of IoT and AI to automatically optimize traffic light duration based on actual vehicle density, reducing congestion by up to 40%.
landing.featuresSubtitle: Our comprehensive features are designed to handle the complexities of modern urban traffic with high precision.
landing.realtimeMonitoringDesc: Monitor 4 or more intersections directly through a centralized dashboard. Full visibility of road conditions every second.
landing.adaptiveAIDesc: Automatic green light timing adjustment. AI analyzes vehicle counts and dynamically prioritizes congested lanes.
landing.smartAnalyticsDesc: In-depth data reports. Historical traffic patterns to assist decision-makers in long-term city planning.
landing.failSafeModeDesc: Guaranteed safety. System automatically returns to safe default timing settings if IoT sensors experience connection disruptions.
landing.ourMission: Our Mission to Revolutionize Urban Mobility
landing.aboutDescription1: ASTRAEA is a smart traffic management system that integrates artificial intelligence with autonomous drone fleets. We believe technology can solve increasingly complex modern transportation challenges.
landing.aboutDescription2: Our platform is designed to help city governments and transportation authorities monitor, analyze, and optimize traffic flow in real-time, creating safer and more efficient roadways for everyone.
landing.teamSubtitle: Our dedication to creating technology solutions with real impact for society.

sections.title: User Guide
sections.subtitle: Learn how to use the Smart Traffic Light system

overview.title: System Overview
overview.subtitle: Introduction to Adaptive Traffic Monitoring System
overview.whatIsDesc: is an integrated IoT solution designed to monitor and manage traffic in real-time using ESP32 sensors, cloud analytics, and modern web dashboard.

features.realtimeDesc: Monitor traffic flow directly via MQTT WebSocket
features.advancedDesc: Data visualization with interactive charts and statistics
features.remoteDesc: Configure traffic light duration from dashboard
features.autoNotifDesc: Alerts via Telegram and Email for traffic conditions

arch.iotLayerDesc: IR and Ultrasonic sensors for vehicle detection
arch.mqttBrokerDesc: Real-time communication between devices and server
arch.pythonSubscriberDesc: Data processing and storage to DynamoDB
arch.dynamodbDesc: Storage for telemetry, device status, and notifications
arch.nextjsDashboardDesc: Web interface for monitoring and control

requirements.title: System Requirements
requirements.subtitle: Required Hardware and Software
requirements.importantNoteDesc: For production deployment, ensure AWS account has sufficient credits (estimated $10-20/month). MQTT Broker runs on AWS EC2 (3.25.72.124). Complete documentation available in PANDUAN_PENGGUNAAN.md

installation.title: Installation & Setup
installation.subtitle: Step-by-step installation guide
installation.totalTimeDesc: Dashboard: 15 minutes • Python: 10 minutes • ESP32: 20 minutes • Total: ±45 minutes

dashboardGuide.title: Using Dashboard
dashboardGuide.subtitle: Dashboard navigation and features guide
dashboardGuide.manualControlStep1: 1. Turn off Auto Mode in Traffic Control Panel
dashboardGuide.manualControlNote: Manual control only works when Auto Mode is OFF!

intersectionManagement.deleteWarningText: Deletion is permanent and cannot be undone!

analyticsGuide.title: Traffic Analytics
analyticsGuide.subtitle: In-depth data visualization and analysis
analyticsGuide.vehicleVolumeDesc: Line chart of vehicle volume per hour or per day with lane filter.
analyticsGuide.queueDistributionDesc: Pie chart of Level 0 (Smooth), Level 1 (Moderate), Level 2 (Heavy) distribution.
analyticsGuide.queueHourlyHeatmapDesc: Heatmap of queue intensity per hour to identify peak hours.
analyticsGuide.queueLevelByHourDesc: Stacked bar chart of queue level distribution per hour.
analyticsGuide.greenDurationChartDesc: Effectiveness of green light duration vs vehicle volume.
analyticsGuide.effectivenessTableDesc: Table of light effectiveness score per lane (0-100).
analyticsGuide.exportDescCont: at top right to download data to CSV. File can be opened with Excel or Google Sheets.

iotConfigGuide.title: IoT Device Configuration
iotConfigGuide.subtitle: Remote configuration for ESP32
iotConfigGuide.greenTimeDesc: Default green light duration when not adaptive.
iotConfigGuide.adaptiveModeOffDesc: Fixed duration according to Green Time
iotConfigGuide.saveConfigStep4: Command sent to ESP32 via MQTT topic:
iotConfigGuide.saveConfigStep5: ESP32 receives and applies new configuration

userManagement.title: User Management
userManagement.subtitle: Role-based access control
userManagement.addNewUserStep3: Fill: Name, Email, Password, Role, Status
userManagement.editUserDesc: Click Edit icon (pencil), modify data, then Save Changes.

notificationsGuide.title: Notifications & Alerts
notificationsGuide.subtitle: Setup Telegram & Email notifications
notificationsGuide.telegramStep6: Enter Bot Token and Chat ID in
notificationsGuide.cooldownDesc: To prevent spam, notifications have a cooldown of
notificationsGuide.cooldownDescCont: . This means the same alert will not be sent again within 5 minutes.

esp32Setup.subtitle: Wiring and uploading code to ESP32

troubleshootingGuide.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshootingGuide.subtitle: Common problem solutions
troubleshootingGuide.mqttDisconnectedSolution1: Check EC2 status in AWS Console
troubleshootingGuide.wifiIssueSolution1: Ensure SSID and password are correct
troubleshootingGuide.wifiIssueSolution2: ESP32 only supports WiFi 2.4 GHz (not 5 GHz)
troubleshootingGuide.wifiIssueSolution3: Distance from ESP32 to router < 10 meters
troubleshootingGuide.awsErrorCauseDesc: Wrong AWS credentials or no permissions
troubleshootingGuide.noDataCheck1: ESP32 connected to WiFi? (check Serial Monitor)
troubleshootingGuide.noDataCheck5: Dashboard connected to MQTT? (green status banner)
troubleshootingGuide.irSensor1: Check wiring: VCC → 5V, GND → GND, OUT → GPIO
troubleshootingGuide.irSensor2: Test with hand in front of sensor (LED should light up)
troubleshootingGuide.ultrasonicSensor1: VCC → 5V, GND → GND, TRIG → GPIO, ECHO → GPIO
troubleshootingGuide.ultrasonicSensor3: Ensure no obstacles in front of sensor during test
troubleshootingGuide.contactSupport: If the issue persists, contact technical support:
troubleshootingGuide.documentationDesc: For more detailed guide, please read the complete documentation files:
